sections.paragraph:
στέγω aor. inf. στέξαι (Sir 8:17) (Aeschyl. et al.; ins, pap; freq. in the sense of covering or enclosing in such a way as to keep someth. undesirable from coming in, as water into a ship)

① to keep confidential, cover, pass over in silence (Eur., Electra 273 τἀμὰ ἔπη; Thu. 6, 72, 5; Polyb. 8, 14, 5 τὸν λόγον; Sir 8:17; Jos., Vi. 225; Field, Notes 177f), so perh. ἡ ἀγάπη πάντα στέγει 1 Cor 13:7 of love that throws a cloak of silence over what is displeasing in another person (Harnack, SBBerlAk 1911, 147; but s. 2).

② to bear up against difficulties, bear, stand, endure (Aeschyl. et al.; Polyb.; Diod S; Plut.; SIG 700, 23; Philo, In Flacc. 64 στέγειν τὰς ἐνδείας) τὶ someth. πάντα 1 Cor 9:12; perh. (s. 1) 13:7 (GWhitaker, ‘Love Springs No Leak’: Exp. 8th ser., 21, 1921, 126ff). Abs. (PGrenf I, 1, 18 [II b.c.]; POxy 1775, 10 καὶ ἔστεξα, ἕως ἔλθῃς) μηκέτι στέγων since I could not bear it any longer 1 Th 3:5; cp. vs. 1.—B. 849. DELG. M-M. TW. Spicq.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

someth. someth. = something

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Field, Notes Field, Notes = Notes on the Translation of the NT

perh. perh. = perhaps

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Exp. Exp. = Expositor—List 6

ser. ser. = series

PGrenf PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 942
